name: Rye (food medium)
basis: per 100 g whole rye grain (Secale cereale), fresh/as-is weight (~11% moisture)

components.name: starch
components.source: Nutrition Journal 2017 doi:10.1186/s12937-017-0246-5; GLNC rye factsheet (whole rye 56-70% starch of dry matter)
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source. 56-70% of dry matter; ~55 g/100g fresh at ~11% moisture (range wide by cultivar).

components.name: total fructans (kestose/nystose-series inulin-type)
components.source: Karppinen et al., Fructan content of rye and rye products (Finnish rye 4.6-6.6 g/100g); whole-rye literature 1.7-3.9 g/100g dry matter
components.evidence: Second most abundant fermentable carbohydrate after starch; major microbial substrate. Reported 1.7-6.6 g/100g across cultivars; midpoint ~4.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Dominant free disaccharide in rye grain.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Not detected in intact grain (present in USDA current list; near zero unless malted/germinated).

components.name: raffinose
components.source: Rakha et al.; rye carbohydrate reviews (raffinose-series present, typically 0.3-0.6 g/100g)
components.evidence: Raffinose-family oligosaccharide reported in rye but not individually quantified in the USDA panel; present, amount uncertain.

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: Rye Flour and Rye Bran review, Processes 2022, 10, 293 doi:10.3390/pr10020293 (wholemeal rye flour 0.64 +/- 0.05 g/100g DW)
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid in whole rye - was entirely missing from FooDB profile.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Processes 2022, 10, 293 doi:10.3390/pr10020293 (wholemeal rye flour 0.16 +/- 0.06 g/100g DW)
components.evidence: Second organic acid; missing from FooDB profile.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Processes 2022, 10, 293 doi:10.3390/pr10020293 (rye bran 0.471 g/100g DW; bran-enriched)
components.evidence: Present; the 0.471 g/100g value is for rye BRAN. Whole-grain level lower/unquantified; included as present.

components.name: lactic acid
components.evidence: Minor organic acid detected in rye flour; present, unquantified.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Protein (mostly prolamins/secalins + glutelins). Amino acid values below are protein-bound totals.

components.name: L-cystine
components.source: USDA FDC 168884 (present; ~0.2 g/100g typical, not returned in this fetch)
components.evidence: Present in USDA amino-acid panel; amount not captured in this extraction.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.source: USDA FDC 168884 / whole-grain betaine literature (rye is betaine-rich, ~100-150 mg/100g)
components.evidence: Genuine and abundant in rye; amount not captured in this fetch.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Consolidates the three duplicate FooDB iron entries (Iron, Iron Fe3+, Fe2+ mitochondria).

components.name: copper
components.evidence: Present; amount not captured in this fetch.

components.name: selenium
components.source: USDA FDC 168884 (Italian landrace 33 ug/100g, PMC11988677 - soil-dependent)

components.name: total dietary fiber (arabinoxylan + beta-glucan + cellulose)
components.evidence: Arabinoxylan is the dominant NSP of rye; largely non-fermentable by minimal media organisms but relevant for fiber-degraders.

components.name: ferulic acid
components.source: Andersson et al.; rye phenolic literature (rye is the richest cereal in ferulic acid, ~50-100 mg/100g, mostly cell-wall bound)
components.evidence: Hallmark rye phenolic; mostly ester-bound to arabinoxylan.

remove.name: Creatine cytosol
remove.reason: Animal muscle metabolite; not synthesized by cereal grain. FooDB predicted/implausible in a plant matrix.

remove.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
remove.reason: Animal-specific sulfonic amino acid; absent from plant grains. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed (animal) vitamin A; rye contains provitamin carotenoids (trace), not retinol. Remove; keep beta-carotene as trace phytochemical.

remove.name: Serotonin
remove.reason: Predicted trace biogenic amine, not a growth nutrient; FooDB noise for a growth medium.

remove.name: Dopamine
remove.reason: Predicted trace catecholamine; not a quantitatively relevant growth nutrient. FooDB noise.

remove.name: 4-Hydroxyproline / cis-4-Hydroxyproline
remove.reason: Collagen-associated (animal) imino acid; not a free grain nutrient. FooDB predicted artifact.

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria / Iron (Fe3+) (duplicate iron entries)
remove.reason: Compartment/oxidation-state duplicates of the same nutrient; consolidated into a single 'iron' component (2.67 mg/100g).

remove.name: Cobalt (Co2+)
remove.reason: Ultratrace; retained only as B12 cofactor context. No plant B12; drop as a defined growth nutrient (keep as trace only if the model needs Co).

missing_added: quinic acid (0.64 g/100g - dominant organic acid, was absent)
citric, lactic, acetic, succinic, oxalic acids (present)
starch amount (~55 g/100g - dominant carbon source)
total fructans (~4 g/100g - major fermentable oligosaccharide)
individual sugar amounts (sucrose 0.71, glucose 0.16, fructose 0.11 g/100g)
quantitative vitamins B1/B2/B3/B5/B6/B9, choline, E, K
quantitative minerals (K, P, Mg, Ca, Fe, Zn, Mn, Se)
keep_uncovered: alkylresorcinols: 5-heptadecyl/nonadecyl/heneicosyl/tricosyl/pentacosyl-1,3-benzenediol, Adipostatin A (genuine rye whole-grain markers, no BiGG met)
ferulic acid dehydrodimers: 5-5', 8-4', 5-8', 8-8'/benzofuran dehydrodiferulic acids (genuine rye arabinoxylan cross-links)
lignans: secoisolariciresinol, matairesinol, lariciresinol, pinoresinol, medioresinol, syringaresinol (genuine rye lignans)
phenolic acids: p-coumaric, trans-sinapic, caffeic, syringic, vanillic, 4-hydroxybenzoate, ferulate, vanillate (genuine)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is STARCH (~55 g/100g), followed by fructans (~4 g/100g) and quinic acid (0.64 g/100g, the dominant organic acid and previously wholly missing); free sugars are minor (sucrose 0.71 > glucose 0.16 > fructose 0.11 g/100g). Rye is protein-modest (10.3 g, Glx/Pro-rich, Lys-limiting), lipid-poor (1.6 g, linoleic-dominant), and mineral-rich (K, P, Mg, Mn, Zn). Organic acids (quinic, malic, citric, plus minor lactic/acetic/succinic/oxalic) were the key growth-relevant gap in the FooDB profile and have been added from Processes 2022 doi:10.3390/pr10020293. Removed animal/predicted FooDB artifacts (creatine, taurine, retinol, hydroxyproline, serotonin, dopamine) and consolidated duplicate iron entries.
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 168884 (Rye grain, SR Legacy) - macros, individual sugars, full amino-acid profile, vitamins, minerals, fatty acids
Rye Flour and Rye Bran: New Perspectives for Use, Processes 2022, 10, 293, doi:10.3390/pr10020293 - organic acids (quinic 0.64, malic 0.16 g/100g flour; citric 0.471 g/100g bran)
Karppinen et al. / Rakha et al., fructan content of rye (1.7-6.6 g/100g dry matter)
Nutrition Journal 2017, doi:10.1186/s12937-017-0246-5 and GLNC - whole-rye starch 56-70% of dry matter
Nutritional Values and Biochemical Traits of Rye (Secale cereale L.) Seeds, PMC11988677 - cross-check protein/lipid/mineral/AA/fatty-acid ranges (Se 33 ug/100g landrace)
Rye phenolic/ferulic acid literature (Andersson et al.) - ferulic acid, alkylresorcinols, lignans
